---
title: "7. Final remarks"
date-modified: last-modified
---

::: {.callout-note .draft}
**Draft scaffold.** Final paragraph should pull together the three R01
aims into a coherent dissemination message. Add a "what's next" call to
action and the support-email alias once one exists.
:::

## What you have now

You have read about — and can run — Two-Stage TMLE in two settings:

1. **Foundational** ([Chapter 5](05_R_foundational.qmd)) — missing
   outcomes in a CRT.
2. **Mediated** ([Chapter 6](06_R_mediated.qmd)) — the multilevel-
   mediation-missing data problem when screening sits on the causal path.

Both share the same Stage 2: a cluster-level TMLE with Adaptive
Pre-specification, robust and efficient with few independent units.

## What's coming

::: {.roadmap-step}
#### Year 2 — Effect heterogeneity (Aim 1B)

A new chapter on **data-driven identification and evaluation of effect
modifiers**, using cross-validated cluster-level CATE TMLE and the
step-down maxT multiple-testing procedure.
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
#### Year 2 — The Roadmap Shiny walker

A point-and-click web app, embedded right inside this site, that walks
you through Causal Roadmap steps 1–6 and emits a fully-annotated R
Markdown analysis file you can knit.
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
#### Year 3 — SAPPHIRE worked example (Aim 2)

A real-data worked example on the SAPPHIRE trial — 16 communities,
~80 000 men and women in rural Kenya and Uganda — showing how Two-Stage
TMLE refines the primary effectiveness analysis of a dynamic-choice HIV
prevention strategy.
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
#### Year 4 — Peer-led DCP worked example (Aim 2)

A second real-data worked example on the Peer-led DCP trial — 14 clinic
catchment areas, ~560 women working in bars and guesthouses in
southwestern Uganda — showing the same methodology in a new
implementation context.
:::

::: {.roadmap-step}
#### Year 5 — Refinements

Final external review board feedback, long-tail FAQ, and an archival
snapshot deposited at Zenodo for long-term reproducibility.
:::

## Get involved

The methods, the code, and this site are all open. Contributions and
critiques are welcome:

- **Code or method issue?** Open a ticket at
  <https://github.com/LauraBalzer/TwoStageTMLE/issues>.
- **Suggestion for the site?** Open a PR or an issue at the site's repo.
- **Question that doesn't fit either?** [Support email — TBD].

## Citation

If you use the methods or code in your work, please cite:

> Balzer L.B., van der Laan M., Ayieko J., Kamya M., Chamie G., Schwab J.,
> Havlir D., Petersen M. (2021). *Two-Stage TMLE to Reduce Bias and Improve
> Efficiency in Cluster Randomized Trials.* arXiv:2106.15737.

For the mediated Stage 1 estimator:

> Nakato J., Balzer L.B. (2026). *Methods for evaluating overall
> effectiveness of cluster-level strategies aiming to improve reach and
> health outcomes.* [arXiv: forthcoming.]

## Acknowledgements

This work is supported by the National Institutes of Health.
